LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANITA TSE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT- DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH EXPECTED INHERITANCE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS HAVE AN ADEQUATE REMEDY IN PROBATE AND HAVE NOT BEEN DEPRIVED OF ACCESS TO THE PROBATE SYSTEM, THEY CANNOT PURSUE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH EXPECTED INHERITANCE. (BECKWITH V. DAHL (2012) 205 CAL.APP.4TH 1039, 1052, 1056) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES A. BARRINGER OF THE BARRINGER LAW, TEL. NO. 321-1812 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. KEVIN M. RODRIGUEZ OF HOPKINS & CARLEY, TEL. NO. (408) 286-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ANITA TSE.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT ANITA TSE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT- Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action for intentional interference with expected inheritance is sustained without leave to amend. The Court grants Defendants' request for judicial notice. Because Plaintiffs have an adequate remedy in probate and have not been deprived of access to the probate system, they cannot pursue a cause of action for intentional interference with expected inheritance. (Beckwith v. Dahl (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 1039, 1052, 1056) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-16-2013 Continued To Nov-20-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-30-2013 Off Calendar
